summary: Drew DeVault announces he will begin working full-time on free software starting in February 2019, funded by donations and sr.ht subscriptions. He acknowledges the financial risk, noting his income will be negative for a while and he's relying on savings and runway. His primary focus will be sr.ht (his main funding source), with secondary attention to Alpine Linux on RISC-V and neglected projects like aerc. He thanks contributors and donors while being transparent that sustainability isn't guaranteed yet.
key_points: Sr.ht paid users provide the majority of funding that makes this possible, making it the primary focus
The decision is financially risky — income will be negative and he's burning runway with hopes of growing subscriptions before savings run out
Sway and wlroots are deprioritized since sway 1.0 is nearly complete, allowing focus on neglected projects like aerc
He frames this as a community achievement, crediting donors, paying alpha users, and volunteer contributors
money_quotes: Today I'm proud to tell you that, thanks to everyone's support, I'll be working on free software full-time starting in February.
I don't want to make grandiose promises right away, but I'm confident that increasing my commitment to open source to this degree is going to have a major impact on my projects.
My income is going to be negative for a while.
I plan on working my ass off before my runway ends to earn the additional subscriptions to sr.ht and donations that will make this decision sustainable in the long term.
If that doesn't happen before I get near the end of my runway, I'll have to start looking for different work again.
If you were impressed by what I built in my spare time, just wait until you see what we can accomplish now!
key_insight: Sustainable full-time free software work is possible through a combination of community donations and a paid service like sr.ht, but it requires accepting real financial risk and burning personal runway.
